summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, including bundling the server inside the app and setting up automated GitHub Actions CI/CD to build and publish releases.
summary.outcome: Created a full release pipeline with GitHub Actions, bundled the Node.js server inside the Electron app via utilityProcess, set up a homebrew-gossamer tap repo, and manually fixed the v1.0.7 cask formula with correct SHA256s after diagnosing a version mismatch issue.

repo: The project has two separate package.json files: root (server/gossamer-serve) and app/ (Electron UI) — they were originally independent programs
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT, which constrains how the server can be bundled (path structure must be preserved)
utilityProcess.fork() requires a CJS entry point, necessitating a server-launcher.cjs wrapper to bridge into the ESM server
prisma was in devDependencies but is needed at runtime for pushSchema — must be in dependencies for production builds
The git remote is at github.com/nosman/gossamer; homebrew tap is at github.com/nosman/homebrew-gossamer
All packaging changes live on the README branch, not main

code.finding: CJS wrapper that uses dynamic import() to load the ESM server, bridging utilityProcess.fork() (CJS-only) to the ESM server entry
ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL env var presence gates whether the main process starts the bundled server — dev workflow is unchanged
Must include a repository field pointing to github.com/nosman/gossamer or electron-builder cannot detect the repo for publish config
Must include permissions: contents: write at the job level — GitHub Actions defaults to read-only, blocking release creation
electron-builder must be run with --publish never; the softprops/action-gh-release step handles uploading, not electron-builder itself

workflow: GitHub Actions built-in GITHUB_TOKEN defaults to read-only permissions; contents: write must be explicitly declared for release creation
Fine-grained PATs for cross-repo pushes (homebrew tap) need only Contents: Read and write + Metadata: Read on the target repo
Homebrew Cask files must live in a Casks/ subdirectory of the tap repo
npm ci fails if package-lock.json is out of sync with package.json — must run npm install locally and commit the updated lock file when adding dependencies
electron-builder derives DMG filenames from the version field in app/package.json, not the git tag — these must be kept in sync to avoid 404s in the cask formula

friction: package-lock.json was out of sync after adding electron-builder, causing npm ci to fail in CI — required a worktree commit to push to the README branch without switching branches
electron-builder could not detect the GitHub repo because .git is in the parent of app/, requiring an explicit repository field in app/package.json
electron-builder was attempting to publish DMGs itself (skipping because not on a tag), conflicting with the softprops upload step — needed --publish never flag
GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN lacked contents: write permission by default, blocking release creation
Version mismatch between app/package.json (1.0.0) and git tag (v1.0.7) caused DMG filenames to be Gossamer-1.0.0-*.dmg while the cask formula expected v1.0.7 URLs, resulting in download 404s
The cask auto-update script in CI pushed correct SHA256s but the local copy diverged, causing a rebase conflict that had to be aborted
Network outage mid-session caused several push failures, requiring manual pushes once connectivity restored
open_items: Workflow version sync fix (bumping app/package.json version to match git tag before building) was written but not confirmed pushed — verify this landed
App is unsigned (no Apple Developer cert) — users must manually allow via System Settings → Privacy & Security on first launch; requires $99/yr Apple Developer account to fix
Considered but deferred: consolidating app/ and root server code into a single package — currently extraResources uses ../ paths to reach server artifacts
Revert the README branch workflow trigger back to tags-only was done, but should confirm the README branch is fully merged or cleaned up
The gossamer-serve standalone CLI binary entry could still be preserved in package.json for headless use — not addressed
